text.title: Stakeholder Management Core Rules
text.summary: Stakeholder register + power × interest × attitude grid + per-stakeholder engagement cadence + decision-rights map for PMs running cross-functional features and pre-launch GTM.

rule.statement: Stakeholder register MUST tag each stakeholder with power (high/low), interest (high/low), and attitude (supporter/neutral/blocker).
rule.rationale: Two-axis grids miss the attitude axis.

rule.statement: Each (power × interest × attitude) quadrant MUST have a written engagement cadence (frequency, channel, content).
rule.rationale: Without cadence, high-power blockers get the same comms as low-power supporters.

rule.statement: Each cross-functional decision type MUST name decision-rights (D=decide / I=input / V=veto). Ambiguous rights produce decision paralysis.
rule.rationale: Re-litigated decisions consume calendar time and erode trust.

rule.statement: Escalation triggers MUST be pre-registered: scope > X, cost > Y, risk severity > Z.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent escalation either over-escalates or under-escalates.

rule.statement: PMs MUST run a written upward-comms cadence (weekly status, monthly review) regardless of vibe.
rule.rationale: Silence breeds anxiety in exec sponsors and triggers reactive interventions.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — single-team product; pre-PMF with no exec sponsors; agency project with one client contact; recent register without org change), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
